pi_items.source: setup_task.sh: Flight.java (line 52) and FlightScheduler.java (line 97) both import java.util.Date. This is the legacy date class being replaced by java.time.LocalDateTime
setup_task.sh: FlightScheduler.java (line 96) and FlightSchedulerTest.java (line 140) both import java.util.Calendar. This is the legacy calendar class being replaced

setup_task.sh: FlightSchedulerTest.java (line 142) imports java.util.GregorianCalendar. The test uses 'new GregorianCalendar(2023, Calendar.OCTOBER, 1, 14, 0)' at lines 151, 175, 185 to create date objects

Java Time API specification (Java 8+): LocalDateTime is the standard modern API for handling date and time without timezone. Verified in verifier.py lines 56, 78, 94 which check for 'java.time.LocalDateTime', 'plusMinutes', 'LocalDateTime.of', and 'LocalDateTime.parse' as indicators of proper refactoring

privileged_info_summary: The task generates a synthetic Maven project called 'flight-scheduler' at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/flight-scheduler containing three Java source files. Flight.java initially uses java.util.Date for departure/arrival times; FlightScheduler.java uses java.util.Calendar for time calculations; and FlightSchedulerTest.java uses java.util.GregorianCalendar for test setup. The task requires refactoring all three files to use java.time.LocalDateTime, removing the three forbidden legacy imports (java.util.Date, java.util.Calendar, java.util.GregorianCalendar) and introducing java.time.LocalDateTime. The initial project compiles and contains two test cases (testScheduleArrival and testOverlappingFlights) that must pass after refactoring.
